articles.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend Festival Features Dance Music Headliners in Sunderland
articles.summary: BBC Radio 1's Big Weekend, an annual music festival, will be held in Sunderland from May 22 to 24, with the first day focused on dance music. Headliners Fatboy Slim and Sonny Fodera will lead the lineup, alongside other acts such as FISHER, Clementine Douglas, and MK. The event takes place at Herrington Country Park, attracting tens of thousands of fans. Tickets for Friday went on sale with priority for local residents, featuring general admission at £34.50 and VIP options at £86.00. Fatboy Slim, a British DJ known for 1990s hits like Praise You, is a festival staple, while Sonny Fodera brings an emotive house sound. Additional performers include Radio 1 DJs and international artists like HorsegiirL and Ewan McVicar. The festival last visited Sunderland in 2005, and this year's event includes about 100 acts over three days. Age restrictions apply, with Friday limited to over-18s, while younger attendees can go with adults on other days. Saturday's headliners will be announced later, with ticket sales continuing for the weekend.

articles.title: UK's Eurovision Risk with an Unconventional Entry
articles.summary: Look Mum No Computer, an experimental musician, has been selected by the BBC to represent the UK at the Eurovision Song Contest with his song Eins, Zwei, Drei. The song is described as wacky and unconventional, featuring German lyrics and references to English desserts, deliberately avoiding the polished pop style of past entries. Sam Battle, the musician's real name, expresses surprise at the BBC's choice, noting they are taking a risk due to the UK's recent poor performance in Eurovision, including zero public votes for recent entrants. He adopts a philosophical approach, acknowledging potential voting biases and aiming to be authentic to connect with audiences. The song's unique charm, inspired by blends like Blur and Falco, is designed to stand out in a competitive field. Battle's lyrics also touch on themes like office drudgery, adding personal depth to the energetic track.

articles.title: Tennis Caught in Crossfire: The Fujairah Tournament Crisis
articles.summary: During an ATP Challenger tennis tournament in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, players found themselves in the midst of a Middle East conflict. Iranian drone and missile attacks targeted the UAE in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iran, leading to explosions and a fire at a nearby oil terminal. Despite government advisories to seek shelter, the ATP proceeded with the tournament after consulting local authorities, deeming it safe. Players, including Australian James McCabe, expressed fear and confusion, hearing explosions and seeing fighter jets overhead. On Tuesday, they fled the courts when the oil terminal was set ablaze by drone debris. The tournament was eventually canceled after a 10-hour delay, along with a second scheduled event, causing logistical chaos as players scrambled for flights. Two players, Marat Sharipov and Sergey Fomin, along with their coaches, remained stranded in the UAE. The incident raised questions about the ATP's decision-making and prioritization of safety during geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: Cillian Murphy Reflects on Peaky Blinders' Legacy in New Film Release
articles.summary: Cillian Murphy, the Irish actor renowned for his role as Tommy Shelby, expresses humility over the enduring passion for Peaky Blinders as he returns in the feature film Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man. This film marks his reprisal of the Birmingham gangster character 13 years after the TV series debut, highlighting how fan-driven popularity transformed the show into a phenomenon without initial promotion. Murphy credits fans for their deep investment in the characters, viewing the movie as a return on that investment. The release follows a four-year hiatus after the series finale, during which Murphy achieved acclaim with awards for Oppenheimer. New cast members like Barry Keoghan, Rebecca Ferguson, and Tim Roth join the project, with creator Steven Knight praising the exceptional cast and loyal fanbase, noting their spontaneous support through tattoos and gatherings. The film will have a two-week cinema run before streaming on Netflix, aiming to foster in-person community among fans. Set in 1940 during World War Two, it depicts Tommy Shelby in isolation, grappling with his past and being drawn back into the gangster world by his son, offering a therapeutic narrative through book-writing.

articles.title: Britney Spears Arrested for DUI in California
articles.summary: Britney Spears, the renowned pop star, was arrested in California on suspicion of driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs. The California Highway Patrol detained her after she was reported for driving her BMW erratically at high speed on US-101. Officers noted signs of impairment and conducted field sobriety tests. She was released from jail early the next day and is scheduled to appear in Ventura County Superior Court on May 4. Her representative described the incident as inexcusable and stated that Britney will comply with the law and seek help, with her family planning to support her well-being. The case remains under investigation, with chemical test results pending. Following the arrest, Spears deleted her Instagram account. Known for hits like "Toxic" and "Baby One More Time," she had previously announced she would not return to the music industry, though she expressed interest in performing in the UK and Australia. The arrest highlights ongoing challenges in her public life.

articles.title: British Tennis Advances at Indian Wells with Wins and Challenges
articles.summary: British tennis players Sonay Kartal and Jacob Fearnley advanced to the second round of the Indian Wells tournament with straight-set victories. Kartal, the British women's number two, defeated Thailand's Lanlana Tararudee 6-4, 6-4, overcoming deficits in both sets. She will next face American 20th seed Emma Navarro. Fearnley, the British men's number three, beat Bosnia's Damir Dzumhur 6-3, 6-3 and is set to play American seventh seed Taylor Fritz. However, fellow Briton Fran Jones lost to American qualifier Kayla Day in straight sets. Emma Raducanu will begin her tournament on Friday against Russian Anastasia Zakharova. Additionally, Venus Williams, a seven-time Grand Slam champion, suffered her eighth consecutive singles loss to French player Diane Parry, citing windy conditions as a factor. The article covers early-round results and player reactions at this prestigious ATP and WTA event in California.
